title: Sweet Smoked Basque-French Chicken Coq Au Vin
description: Buttery, crispy, indulgent French dish featuring sweet smoked paprika, grilled to perfection.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with salt, pepper, and sweet smoked paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the chicken thighs until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the sliced onions to the skillet and cook until they are softened and caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the button mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they are tender, about 3 minutes.
Add the red wine and chicken broth to the skillet, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Bring the liquid to a simmer and cook until it has reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
Add the butter to the skillet and stir until it is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Add the flour to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Return the chicken to the skillet and stir to coat with the sauce.
Simmer the chicken in the sauce until it is cooked through, about 10 minutes.
Serve the chicken hot, garnished with fresh thyme.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
Serve hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
Serve with crusty bread or over egg noodles for a hearty meal.
Garnish with fresh thyme and serve with a side of sautéed spinach for a complete meal.
Serve with a side of roasted vegetables for a well-rounded meal.
Serve with a side salad for a light and refreshing meal.
Serve with a side of quinoa for a nutritious and filling meal.
Serve with a side of roasted potatoes for a comforting and satisfying meal.
